mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it ideal for applications requiring reliable storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the flexibility and scalability of storage options.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Rook operator using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-system' namespace using the specified chart version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rook-system

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

```

> Creates a namespace for Rook.
> Installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD) to define the cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/ceph/cluster.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample Ceph cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify Ceph Cluster Status
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pod

kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

```

> Lists all pods in the rook-system namespace to verify their status.
> Executes a command in the Rook Ceph tools pod to check the Ceph cluster status.
commands: kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may result in data loss if the Ceph cluster is not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-system

kubectl delete namespace rook-system

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> Deletes the rook-system namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your Ceph cluster data. This step involves draining the nodes if necessary, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-system get pod

```

> Check the current status before upgrading.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph operator to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status
helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the required permissions are not set for the Kubernetes service account.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the necessary RBAC permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph cluster status shows 'Health: WARNING'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there may be issues with the Ceph OSDs or MONs.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the OSD and MON pods for errors and resolve any issues.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen due to insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that there are enough resources available in the cluster for the pods to schedule.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available monitors'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ceph monitors are not running or reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the monitor pods and ensure they are running correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for CephFS snapshots and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy storage classes.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.18.x, ensure to migrate any legacy storage classes to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-system get pod' showing all pods in the 'Running' state, and 'ceph status' should report 'HEALTH_OK'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3
kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
